name: Shrimp Loaf
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb puff pastry, thawed or homemade
3 large tomatoes, peeled, seeded, and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the onion in 1/2 cup of olive oil and butter until soft and translucent.
- Add bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
- Stir in minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
2. Mix in chopped tomatoes, cover pan, and simmer until tomatoes become almost a paste.
- Stir occasionally while cooking.
3. Add shrimp to the sauce along with salt and pepper.
- Cook just until shrimp turn opaque.
4. Make a roux by heating the remaining olive oil, adding butter until foamy, and stirring in flour.
5. Combine the shrimp and sauce with the roux mixture and stir until thickened.
6. Chill the mixture for at least 4 hours.
7. Roll out dough very thin into a rectangle.
8. Place the shrimp mixture in the center of the dough.
9. Dampen the edges with water, fold the dough around the filling to shape it like a loaf, and chill for 1 hour.
10. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
11. Place the loaf on a baking sheet, seam side down. Make two small holes on top for steam to escape.
12. Decorate with leftover dough if desired.
13. Glaze with egg yolk.
14. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 20 minutes, then reduce heat to 300°F (150°C) and continue baking another 20 to 25 minutes.
15. Serve hot.
